truthAudit.Summary: Of 20 true vulnerabilities, the audited Run 2 answer found 8 fully, 2 partially, and missed 10. One previous finding was a false positive not present in the ground truth. Severity underclaim on the hardcoded credential finding.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Format String Vulnerability in Debug Logger ... printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input);
Items.Notes: Matches finding #4 exactly: format string via runtime log format, same line 238, same CWE-134.
The audited answer never discusses the factorial function at lines 312-319 or the integer overflow in result *= i. No mention of fact() anywhere.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Use-After-Free in Deferred History Flush ... stage_deferred_history_flush(); memory::g_memory_pool.cleanup();
Items.Notes: Matches finding #9 exactly: background tasks capture pool pointers, pool freed immediately after, same lines 883-884.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Command Injection in Config Validation ... std::string command = validator_cmd + " " + config_file; int result = system(command.c_str());
Items.Notes: Matches finding #2 exactly: same function, same lines 563-569, same CWE-78.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Buffer Overflow in evaluate_expression ... if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) { strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()); }
Items.Notes: Matches finding #6 exactly: inverted condition causing unbounded strcpy, same lines 831-835.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Data Race in ThreadSafeCounter ... long long old_value = value_; std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(mutex_);
Items.Notes: Matches finding #10 exactly: value read before acquiring mutex, same lines 482-487.

The audited answer never discusses the EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE backdoor at lines 592-598 or the logic bomb that grants admin access after 5 failed attempts. The admin console finding (#1) was about exec command injection, not this authentication bypass.
The audited answer never discusses path traversal via config_dir_ + filename in load_config. No mention of directory escaping or ../ sequences.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Buffer Overflow in Expression Parser ... buffer_size_ = 256; ... strcpy(expression_buffer_, expr_str.c_str());
Items.Notes: Matches finding #5 exactly: 256-byte buffer, unbounded strcpy from concatenated token string, same lines 366-376.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Hardcoded Authentication Secret ... constexpr char ADMIN_SECRET[] = "SC_ENT_2025_AUTH"; ... severity: Medium
Items.Notes: Found the same hardcoded credential at line 73 but underclaimed severity as Medium when the true severity is High.
The audited answer never discusses generate_session_token, the mt19937 PRNG, or the 4-digit token space (1000-9999).
The audited answer never discusses the static std::tm cached_time in log_debug_message or the thread-unsafe localtime call at lines 230-231.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Command Injection in Audit Event Logging ... std::string command = "sh -c \"echo Admin override logged >> " + audit_path + "\""; system(command.c_str());
Items.Notes: Found the same code path (lines 622-633) but characterized it as command injection (CWE-78) rather than insecure temporary file handling (CWE-377). The mechanism — temp dir from environment fed into system() — is the same vulnerability, just misattributed.
The audited answer never discusses the persistent authenticated_ and session_token_ state that survives across operations with no expiration or invalidation.
The audited answer never discusses std::stod(tokens[3]) in handle_variable_command or the missing bounds check on tokens.size() before accessing index 3.
The audited answer never discusses the memory pool block split logic at lines 131-141 or the integer underflow when previous_size - size - SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES wraps.

The audited answer never discusses unbounded recursion in parse_expression/parse_factor/parse_factor (nested parentheses) or the MAX_RECURSION_DEPTH limit that is defined but never enforced in the parser.

The audited answer never discusses result_cache_ in ExpressionEvaluator being written to from background tasks (schedule_expression_analysis) while the cache_mutex_ guards nothing in evaluate_cached's concurrent callers.
The audited answer never discusses the time-of-check to time-of-use gap in load_config: filesystem::exists check, then sleep_for(25ms), then ifstream open.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Integer Division by Zero ... return 100 / static_cast<int>(token_length);
Items.Notes: Matches finding #8 exactly: same function, same line 752, same CWE-369.

Corrections.RejectionReason: previous claim is not an exact audited-output quote

Corrections.PreviousClaim: Command Injection in Audit Event Logging (CWE-78)
Corrections.RejectionReason: previous claim is not an exact audited-output quote

Items.Key: command injection in admin console|command injection|cwe-78|critical|0.95|enhanced_calc.cpp|605|609|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|if (command.substr(0, 4) == "exec") { std::string cmd = command.substr(5); system(cmd.c_str()); }|authenticated attackers can execute arbitrary os commands with the privileges of the application process, leading to full system compromise.|an authenticated admin sends an 'exec' command via the admin console.|replace system() with a safe execution api (e.g., popen with restricted whitelisted commands, or std::process) and validate/sanitize all inputs.->command injection in admin console|command injection|cwe-78|critical|0.95|enhanced_calc.cpp|605|609|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|if (command.substr(0, 4) == "exec") { std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str()); }|authenticated attackers can execute arbitrary os commands with the privileges of the application process, leading to full system compromise.|an authenticated admin sends an 'exec <payload>' command via the admin console.|replace system() with a safe execution api and validate inputs against a strict whitelist.

command injection in audit event logging|command injection|cwe-78|high|0.85|enhanced_calc.cpp|622|633|admin::adminconsole::append_override_audit_event|std::string audit_path = std::string(temp_dir ? temp_dir : "/tmp") + "/supercalc_emergency.log"; std::string command = "sh -c \"echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path + "\""; system(command.c_str());|attackers controlling environment variables (tmpdir/temp) can inject shell commands, leading to privilege escalation or data exfiltration.|triggering an admin override while tmpdir or temp contains shell metacharacters.|write audit logs directly using std::ofstream instead of invoking a shell via system().->command injection in audit event logging|command injection|cwe-78|high|0.85|enhanced_calc.cpp|622|633|admin::adminconsole::append_override_audit_event|const char* temp_dir = std::getenv("tmpdir"); std::string audit_path = std::string(temp_dir ? temp_dir : "/tmp") + "/supercalc_emergency.log"; std::string command = "sh -c \"echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path + "\""; system(command.c_str());|attackers controlling environment variables (tmpdir/temp) can inject shell commands, leading to privilege escalation or data exfiltration.|triggering an admin override while tmpdir or temp contains shell metacharacters.|write audit logs directly using std::ofstream instead of invoking a shell via system().
